rawValue.description:
Configures Microsoft Defender Antivirus for robust Windows protection.
Settings:  
Real-Time Protection: On (Behavior, Cloud, Network).  

Scans: Archives, email, network files, scripts, drives, downloads.  

Cloud Protection: High blocking, 50s timeout.  

Remediation: Remove severe/high/moderate threats; block low threats, PUAs.  

Performance: 50% CPU cap, low-priority scans.  

Updates: Hourly signatures, allowed on metered connections.
Scope: All included groups, no exclusions.
Monitor: Event Viewer (Windows Defender > Operational), PowerShell (Get-MpThreatDetection).
Note: Adjust for performance or false positives if needed.
rawValue.name: AMP - Win - ES - Defender Antivirus - D - AV Configuration - v1.12

rawValue.description:
PURPOSE: Enhance security by enabling tamper protection and controlling user access to security settings in Windows Defender Security Center.

EXCLUSIONS: None configured.

NEXT STEPS:  
Monitor tamper protection events in Event Viewer:  
Path: Applications and Services Logs > Microsoft > Windows > Windows Defender > Operational

Use PowerShell: Get-MpPreference to verify tamper protection status.

GOAL: Prevent unauthorized changes to security settings while maintaining user awareness through controlled notifications.  

SECURITY ALIGNMENT: Aligns with CIS Benchmarks and NIST SP 800-53 controls.  

IMPORTANT: Tamper protection requires an active Defender for Endpoint P1/P2 or Business license.
rawValue.name: AMP - Win - ES - Defender Antivirus - D - Security Experience - v1.0

rawValue.description:
PURPOSE: Configure Microsoft Defender Antivirus update channels for pilot testing in pre-production environments to validate engine and platform updates early.

EXCLUSIONS: None configured. Assign only to pilot devices.

NEXT STEPS:
Monitor update deployment via Intune reports or PowerShell (Get-MpComputerStatus).

Validate updates on pilot devices before production rollout.

GOAL: Ensure Defender engine and platform updates are stable and compatible in a controlled setting.

SECURITY ALIGNMENT: Supports phased update deployment best practices.

IMPORTANT: Assign this policy only to pilot devices for testing purposes.
rawValue.name: AMP - Win - ES - Defender Antivirus Updates - Ring 1 - Pilot - v1.0

rawValue.description:
PURPOSE: Configure Microsoft Defender Antivirus update channels for User Acceptance Testing (UAT) in a staged environment to validate engine, platform, and security intelligence updates after the initial gradual release cycle.

EXCLUSIONS: None configured.

NEXT STEPS:
Monitor update deployment via Intune reports or PowerShell (Get-MpComputerStatus).

Validate updates on UAT devices to ensure stability and compatibility.

GOAL: Confirm the stability and effectiveness of Defender updates in a production-like setting before broader deployment.

SECURITY ALIGNMENT: Supports phased update deployment best practices.

IMPORTANT: Assign this policy only to a small, representative subset of production devices (~10%) for UAT purposes.
rawValue.name: AMP - Win - ES - Defender Antivirus Updates - Ring 2 - UAT - v1.0

rawValue.description:
PURPOSE: Deploy stable, validated Microsoft Defender Antivirus updates (engine, platform, and security intelligence) to production devices after testing in earlier rings.  

EXCLUSIONS: None configured.  

NEXT STEPS:  
Monitor update deployment via Intune reports or PowerShell (Get-MpComputerStatus).  

Ensure updates are applied successfully across all targeted devices.

GOAL: Maintain security and stability in production by applying tested Defender updates.  

SECURITY ALIGNMENT: Supports phased update deployment best practices.  

IMPORTANT: Assign this policy to production devices only after validation in earlier rings (Pilot and UAT).
rawValue.name: AMP - Win - ES - Defender Antivirus Updates - Ring 3 - Production - v1.0

rawValue.description:
PURPOSE:
Enforce BitLocker encryption on Windows OS drives using XTS-AES 256-bit encryption, requiring full encryption and TPM usage, with recovery keys stored in AD DS for data protection at rest.

EXCLUSIONS:
None configured (no excluded groups or filters specified).

NEXT STEPS:  
Verify encryption status using Intune reports or PowerShell (manage-bde -status).  

Confirm recovery keys are backed up to AD DS before encryption begins.  

Review compliance for devices without TPM (password or USB key required).

GOAL:
Ensure all OS drives are fully encrypted with robust recovery options, enhancing data security while maintaining manageability.

SECURITY ALIGNMENT:
Aligns with NIST SP 800-53 (SC-28) and CIS Benchmarks for encryption and key management.

IMPORTANT:  
Requires TPM; non-TPM devices need a password or USB key.  

48-digit recovery passwords and key packages stored in AD DS.  

Standard users cannot change PIN/password.
rawValue.name: AMP - Win - ES - Encryption - D - BitLocker (OS Disk) - v1.1

rawValue.description:
PURPOSE:
Enable Personal Data Encryption (PDE) on Windows 11 22H2+ Entra-Joined devices to secure sensitive folders (Pictures, Documents, Desktop) for improved data protection.

EXCLUSIONS:
No excluded groups or filters configured; applies broadly to assigned groups.

NEXT STEPS:  
Check PDE status using Intune reports or PowerShell (Get-PDEStatus).  

Confirm devices run Windows 11 22H2+ and are Entra-Joined (non-Pro/Business SKUs).

GOAL:
Safeguard user data in Pictures, Documents, and Desktop folders with PDE encryption.

SECURITY ALIGNMENT:
Aligns with NIST SP 800-53 (SC-28) for protecting data at rest.

IMPORTANT:  
PDE requires Windows 11 22H2+ on Entra-Joined devices; not supported on Pro/Business SKUs.
rawValue.name: AMP - Win - ES - Encryption - U - Personal Data Encryption - v1.0

rawValue.description:
PURPOSE:
Configure Windows Defender Firewall to enhance network security, reduce threats, and audit network activity across Domain, Private, and Public profiles.

EXCLUSIONS:
None configured. Applies to all assigned devices.

NEXT STEPS:  
Monitor firewall logs (e.g., %SystemRoot%\System32\logfiles\firewall\domainfw.log).  

Review audit logs for filtering platform connections and packet drops.  

Verify settings with PowerShell (Get-NetFirewallProfile).

GOAL:
Enforce strict firewall rules, disable unnecessary features (e.g., stateful FTP), and enable logging and auditing for security monitoring.

SECURITY ALIGNMENT:
Aligns with NIST SP 800-53 (SC-7, AU-2) and CIS Benchmarks for Windows firewall configurations.

IMPORTANT:  
Default inbound action is "Block" for all profiles; configure exceptions as needed.  

Local policy and IPsec merges disabled; settings are centrally managed.  

Stealth mode enabled for added security.
rawValue.name: AMP - Win - ES - Windows Firewall - D - Firewall Configuration - v1.1

rawValue.description:
PURPOSE:
This policy enforces secure authentication using Windows Hello for Business (WHfB), supporting biometric (e.g., facial recognition or fingerprint) or PIN-based methods. It enhances security by requiring anti-spoofing features and Trusted Platform Module (TPM) usage.

EXCLUSIONS:
No exclusions are configured for this policy— it applies to all targeted devices without exceptions.

NEXT STEPS:  
Verify WHfB deployment status using Intune or PowerShell (run Get-WindowsHelloForBusiness).  

Ensure devices have a TPM available to meet compliance requirements.

GOAL:
The goal is to replace traditional passwords with stronger, more user-friendly authentication methods that improve both security and user experience.

SECURITY ALIGNMENT:
This policy aligns with industry standards, including NIST SP 800-63B (Digital Identity Guidelines) and CIS Benchmarks for Windows security.

IMPORTANT:
Devices lacking a TPM will not comply with this policy and may fail authentication.
rawValue.name: AMP - Win - ES - Windows Hello for Business - D - WHfB Configuration - v1.0

rawValue.description: Maintaining a level of parity between Edge and Chrome is difficult, and the OIB Chrome policies were (on purpose) very 'Anti Chrome'. My focus will be to ensure the best set of policies for Edge moving forward, and dropping the Chrome policies. It is my opinion that Edge should be the primary and only browser available in an enterprise environment, and continued efforts by Microsoft to improve the security and managability of Edge for Business backs this up. My recommendation is to use the Edge Management Service to Block other Browsers(https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/deployedge/microsoft-edge-management-service-customizations#block-other-browsers) which creates and deploys an AppLocker policy to block the installation or execution of other browsers on corporate devices.
rawValue.name: AMP - Win - SC - Google Chrome - D - Security - v1.0

rawValue.description:
•	PURPOSE: Monitor without blocking to assess impact.

•	EXCLUSIONS: None configured.

•	NEXT STEPS:
o	Review logs in Event Viewer:
o	Navigate to: Applications and Services Logs > Microsoft > Windows > Windows Defender > Operational
o	Look for Event IDs: 1121 (would-be blocks), 1122 (audit mode triggers)
o	Use PowerShell command: Get-MpThreatDetection for detailed insights

•	GOAL: Identify legitimate activities for exclusions before enforcing rules.

•	SECURITY ALIGNMENT: Supports CIS Benchmarks and NIST SP 800-53 controls.
rawValue.name: AMP - Windows - Standard - Attack Surface Reduction - D - ASR Rules (Audit Mode) - v1.0

rawValue.description:
•	PURPOSE: Enforce ASR rules to actively block or warn against potentially malicious activities, enhancing system security and reducing the attack surface.

•	EXCLUSIONS: None configured.

•	NEXT STEPS:
o	Monitor enforced rules via Event Viewer logs:
o	Path: Applications and Services Logs > Microsoft > Windows > Windows Defender > Operational
o	Key Event IDs: 1121 (blocked actions), 1122 (audit mode triggers)
o	Use PowerShell: Get-MpThreatDetection for detailed threat detection insights

•	GOAL: Secure the system with ASR rules while preserving legitimate functionality.

•	SECURITY ALIGNMENT: Aligns with CIS Benchmarks and NIST SP 800-53.

•	IMPORTANT: This policy should only be assigned after validating the impact via audit mode. Refer to the audit mode policy: AMP - Win - ES - Attack Surface Reduction - D - ASR Rules (Audit Mode) - v1.0 for validation guidance.

rawValue.description:
This Intune policy uses a Start layout configuration to pin the Microsoft 365 Copilot app to the Windows taskbar for devices with the Windows 11 May 2025 preview update, or later update installed. The layout keeps apps in the same position on the taskbar, making them easier to find and use. The policy also includes a PinGeneration setting, which controls what happens if a user unpins the app.

Note: We recommend that you manage this policy in the Admin center, where it was originally created and applied. Go to https://aka.ms/PinCopilotApp to manage this policy.
rawValue.name: Pin Microsoft 365 Copilot app to taskbar: Start layout + PinGeneration

simpleSettingValue.value: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?> <LayoutModificationTemplate xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/Start/2014/LayoutModification" xmlns:defaultlayout="http://schemas.microsoft.com/Start/2014/FullDefaultLayout" xmlns:start="http://schemas.microsoft.com/Start/2014/StartLayout" Version="1"> <LayoutOptions StartTileGroupCellWidth="6" /> <DefaultLayoutOverride> <StartLayoutCollection> <defaultlayout:StartLayout> </defaultlayout:StartLayout> </StartLayoutCollection> </DefaultLayoutOverride> <CustomTaskbarLayoutCollection> <defaultlayout:TaskbarLayout> <taskbar:TaskbarPinList xmlns:taskbar="http://schemas.microsoft.com/Start/2014/TaskbarLayout"> <taskbar:UWA AppUserModelID="Microsoft.MicrosoftOfficeHub_8wekyb3d8bbwe!Microsoft.MicrosoftOfficeHub" PinGeneration="1"/> <taskbar:UWA AppUserModelID="Microsoft.M365Companions_8wekyb3d8bbwe!People" PinGeneration="1"/> <taskbar:UWA AppUserModelID="Microsoft.M365Companions_8wekyb3d8bbwe!Files" PinGeneration="1"/> <taskbar:UWA AppUserModelID="Microsoft.M365Companions_8wekyb3d8bbwe!Calendar" PinGeneration="1"/> </taskbar:TaskbarPinList> </defaultlayout:TaskbarLayout> </CustomTaskbarLayoutCollection> </LayoutModificationTemplate>

rawValue.description:
This Intune policy uses a Start layout configuration to pin the Microsoft 365 Copilot app to the taskbar on Windows 10 and 11 devices. Use this policy for devices that do not support PinGeneration, which controls what happens if a user unpins the app. The layout keeps apps in the same position on the taskbar, making them easier to find and use.

Note: We recommend that you manage this policy in the Admin center, where it was originally created and applied. Go to https://aka.ms/PinCopilotApp to manage this policy.
rawValue.name: Pin Microsoft 365 Copilot app to taskbar: Start layout only
